pairs.question: When and where was the First All-Belarusian Congress held?
pairs.answer: The First All-Belarusian Congress took place in Minsk between 18 and 31 December 1917 (Old Style: 5–18 December).

pairs.question: What was the main role of the First All-Belarusian Congress in Belarusian history?
pairs.answer: The Congress played a pivotal role in consolidating the Belarusian national movement following the Bolshevik Revolution. It advocated for republican governance, opposed territorial division, and supported federal solutions within a democratic framework, though its efforts were ultimately suppressed by local Bolshevik authorities.

pairs.question: What actions did delegates take after the Congress was forcibly disbanded?
pairs.answer: After the Congress was violently dispersed by Bolshevik authorities, key delegates secretly convened on 31 December 1917 and formed the All-Belarusian Council. This body later elected an Executive Committee of seventeen members and continued organizing in secret until the Bolsheviks retreated from Minsk in February 1918.

pairs.question: How did the First All-Belarusian Congress address the issue of Belarusian autonomy?
pairs.answer: The Congress approved a republican form of governance for Belarus and insisted on maintaining national unity without territorial division. However, it did not seek full separation, instead supporting federalization within a broader democratic Russian republic, reflecting a compromise between nationalist and federalist principles.

pairs.question: What was the composition of the Executive Committee formed after the Congress?
pairs.answer: The Executive Committee consisted of seventeen members elected by the All-Belarusian Council. This body continued the work of the Congress in secret, advocating for self-determination until political conditions changed.

pairs.question: Why was the Rada of the Belarusian People's Republic established, and when?
pairs.answer: Following the retreat of Bolshevik forces from Minsk due to the German invasion, the All-Belarusian Council transformed into the Rada of the Belarusian People's Republic. It was officially proclaimed on 21 February 1918 during a brief provisional Belarusian-Polish administration of the city (19–21 February).
